text: Recreational runners maintain similar running paces in half-marathons and marathons (approximately 3.1-3.3 m/s), suggesting pace selection is consistent across distances for experienced amateur runners.
Marathon completion causes approximately double the neuromuscular fatigue of half-marathon completion, as measured by countermovement jump height reduction (25% vs 11%).

Serum myoglobin concentration after a marathon is approximately three times higher than after a half-marathon (564 vs 186 µg/mL), indicating substantially greater muscle fiber damage from the longer distance.
Self-reported muscle pain is significantly higher after marathon vs half-marathon completion (5.5 vs 3.0 AU on a pain scale), reflecting the greater mechanical stress of the longer distance.
Sweat rate during endurance racing is approximately 1.0 L/h for recreational runners and does not differ between half-marathon and marathon distances.
Body mass loss is significantly greater after a marathon than a half-marathon (3.3% vs 1.9%), indicating that hydration strategies need to be more aggressive for the marathon distance.
Sweat sodium concentration in recreational endurance runners ranges from approximately 39-48 mmol/L and does not differ between half-marathon and marathon racing.
